design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate side-scrolling levels while managing limited health and ammunition; defeat waves of gunmen and environmental hazards; progress through themed stages toward final boss confrontation
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity (limited continues, few lives, ammunition management) combined with constant enemy pressure in combat-heavy environments
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Embodying John Spartan from the film, progressing through a futuristic dystopia to confront nemesis Simon Phoenix with an arsenal of weapons
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Unforgiving lives/continues system typical of mid-90s arcade-influenced design; mixing side-scrolling platform/action with brief overhead shooter segments for environmental puzzle-solving; film-licensed game that emphasizes action over narrative

difficulty.lives_system: Very limited lives; player can take multiple hits before death but continues are scarce
difficulty.continues_system: Limited continues available; encourages careful progression
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based checkpoint system; levels must be completed in full or progress is reset
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Cheat codes accessible via pause menu (button sequences) allow skipping to next level or acquiring power-ups, providing optional difficulty modulation

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon variety (handgun, magnum, shotgun, grenades) found as power-ups during levels; health restoration items scattered throughout stages
progression.unlock_mechanism: In overhead segments, must rescue hostages to unlock doors and progress; linear stage advancement from 1996 setting to 2032 future stages
replayability_hooks: Cheat codes reward discovery and experimentation
Multiple weapon types encourage different tactical approaches
Teaser boss battles against Simon Phoenix before final confrontation add dramatic pacing

world_building_techniques: Simplified film plot adapted across stages
Stage progression from 1996 to 2032 establishes temporal narrative arc
Visual design conveys postapocalyptic setting (confirmed as 'colorful and effectively conveying postapocalyptic settings')
Recurring Simon Phoenix encounters create dramatic narrative beats

boss_design.philosophy: Teaser encounters with final nemesis (Simon Phoenix) escalate tension toward climactic cryo-prison confrontation; multiple shorter battles punctuate progression
notable_bosses: Simon Phoenix (multiple teaser battles throughout game)

faction_indicators: John Spartan as law enforcement protagonist vs. Simon Phoenix as criminal antagonist
2032 dystopian society implied through stage progression
danger_signals: Gunmen as primary enemy type establishes militarized threat environment
Cryo-prison setting suggests authoritarian control systems
environmental_storytelling: Postapocalyptic visual design creates sense of decay and future societal collapse
Stage progression from 1996 to 2032 shows temporal transformation
Overhead segments requiring hostage rescue imply civilian populations in danger

sources_used: TCRF (The Cutting Room Floor) - Demolition Man (SNES) technical documentation and development data
Wikipedia - Demolition Man (video game) - gameplay mechanics, design credits, and critical reception
